mechanics.excerpt: For example , the drive often accounts for most of JumpShip" mass, which Iimits the amount cargo and smaller ships can carry: Because of this; JumpShips do not have interplanetary drives Instead_ they rely cargo ships equipped with interplanetary drives, known as DropShips Most JumpShips can carry one or more DropShips, JumpShip can only Jump and from predetermined points in space_ known as jump points_ The standard points are ocated above' and below' the plane of the star system; directly to the north and south of the star' $ poles: These points usually lie hundreds of millions of kilometers from the star; (ar out of its gravity well; even weak gravitational tides can rip JumpShip apart: The jump points remote ocation means that JumpShip passengers and cargo must spend several days aboard DropShip_ traveling between the JumpShip ad its planet of origin or destination; Some JumpShip captains usually those transporting illicit cargos; use pirate jump points These nonstandard points are free from gravitational tides formed by the motion of the sys- tem' planets and moons around its star.
